iti96:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti96:2.1: “Tied by the yoke of sensuality & the yoke of becoming, monks, one is a returner, returning to this state.
iti96:2.2: Untied from the yoke of sensuality but tied by the yoke of becoming, one is a non-returner, not returning to this state.
iti96:2.3: Untied from [both] the yoke of sensuality & from the yoke of becoming, one is an Arahant whose effluents are ended.”
iti96:3.1: Tied by both the yoke of sensuality
iti96:4.2: without reaching the ending of effluents,
iti96:4.3: are tied by the yoke of becoming,
iti96:5.1: While those who’ve cut off doubt
iti96:5.2: have no more conceit or further becoming.
iti96:5.3: They who have reached the ending of effluents, while in the world
